messages.defaultDone: macOS will prompt you to confirm.
messages.defaultConsent: Confirm the switch in System Settings > Desktop & Dock.
messages.defaultUnsupported: Default-browser API is not available on this platform.
messages.templateSaveFailed: Template save failed ({{error}}). Continuing without templates. You can add rules from the Rules tab.

step1.title: Welcome to LinkPilot.
step1.body: Route every link to the right browser, profile, and workspace without thinking about it.

rules.title: Rules engine
rules.detail: Match by host, app, or path.

profiles.title: Profiles
profiles.detail: Send work to Work, fun to Fun.

step2.title: Set LinkPilot as your default browser
step2.body: macOS will show a confirmation dialog. LinkPilot does not render web pages. It just decides which browser to hand each link to.
step2.help: You can change this at any time in System Settings > Desktop & Dock.

step3.title: Pick which browsers LinkPilot can route to

step4.title: Pick your default browser
step4.body: When no rule matches, LinkPilot sends the link here. You can change this later in Settings.
step4.noBrowsers: No browsers detected. Skip this step and configure a default later from the Overview tab.
step4.help: Tip: this is the routing fallback. Step 2 was about making LinkPilot the macOS default — different setting.

step5.title: Start from a template
step5.body: We drafted four rules. Toggle the ones you want; tweak the rest in the Rules tab later.
step5.help: You can edit, reorder, or delete rules any time.
step5.noTemplates: No starter templates match the browsers detected on this Mac. You can still add rules later from the Rules tab.

oauth.label: OAuth -> Keep source
oauth.detail: /oauth/callback paths stay in the originating browser
oauth.note: OAuth redirects must complete in the source browser

banking.label: Banking -> {{browser}}
banking.detail: Personal banking sites stay in Safari for keychain support
